text:
INDEX_PATTERN                       = "[[ if eq .deployment_name "none" ]]uem.gumdrop-ingestion.*[[else]][[ .deployment_name ]].uem.gumdrop-ingestion.*[[end]]"

how to check .deployment_name is none or ""
In this code snippet, you want to check if `.deployment_name` is either "none" or an empty string. Currently, it only checks if it's equal to "none".

To check for both "none" and an empty string (""), you can modify the template as follows:

```
INDEX_PATTERN = "[[ if or (eq .deployment_name "none") (eq .deployment_name "") ]]uem.gumdrop-ingestion.*[[else]][[ .deployment_name ]].uem.gumdrop-ingestion.*[[end]]"
```

This uses the `or` function to combine two conditions:
1. `(eq .deployment_name "none")` - checks if deployment_name equals "none"
2. `(eq .deployment_name "")` - checks if deployment_name is an empty string

If either condition is true, it will use `uem.gumdrop-ingestion.*` without a prefix.
